title: HSE temporary-works governance: bounded construction lessons
evidence_domain: non-bondage United Kingdom construction temporary-works governance
attribution: Contains public sector information published by the Health and Safety Executive and licensed under the Open Government Licence.
robots_enforcement: The National Archives robots file states ai-train=no. No National Archives licence-page wording or substantive body is present in CORPUS.md; that body is used only for rights provenance.
retained_scope: temporary duration does not reduce criticality
validation of generic assumptions against actual site conditions
proprietary systems still require competent load-and-condition assessment
side, atypical and contextual actions can be missed
competence depends on relevant training, experience and task context
design and coordination are distinct roles
excluded_scope: standards text, standard identifiers, legal claims and universal-law advice
numerical examples, calculations, values, dimensions, formulas, capacities, thresholds and sizing
proprietary names, product examples, equipment types, supplier directions and selection
installation, assembly, setup, checking, loading, maintenance, removal, testing, proof-load, design and certification recipes
logos, SVGs, icons, images, illustrations, multimedia, styling and image-derived claims
operational use for ceiling tension, hardpoints, anchors, uplines, body support, bondage or human suspension
